bids.reasoning: This is the opening bid at t=0, so I should start with my most preferred values across all issues. My utility is highest for valueE on every issue, giving me maximum utility of 1.0. I'll open with a strong anchor bid requesting valueE for all issues.

The opponent's opening offer is all valueA across every issue, which gives me minimum utility (0.2 per issue weighted = 0.2 total). Their message claims it's "balanced" but the numbers show it's entirely in their favor. We're very early (t=0.02), so I should stay close to my ideal bid. I'll concede very slightly on the low-weight issues (issueA and issueB) while holding firm on the high-weight issues (issueD, issueE, issueC).

The opponent is firmly sticking to all-valueA bids (utility 0.2 for me on all issues), which is the worst possible offer, while their messages claim it's "balanced" and "win-win" â€” this is contradicted by the numbers. We're very early (t=0.04), so I have plenty of time to negotiate. My previous bid was high utility for me (issueD: valueE, issueE: valueE being most important). I'll make a modest concession on lower-weighted issues (A and B) while holding firm on my top priorities (D and E) and making a small concession on C.

The opponent is holding almost completely firm at all-valueA bids, only conceding one step on issueE (from valueA to valueB). Their messaging is classic "win-win" cheap talk while their offers give me near-zero utility. We're very early (t=0.06), so I should continue making gradual concessions on my low-weight issues (issueA, issueB) while holding firm on my high-weight issues (issueD, issueE). I'll move issueA and issueB down slightly more, keep issueC at valueD, and maintain issueD and issueE at valueE to protect my most important issues.

The opponent has been making minimal concessions - only moving issueE from valueA to valueB (utility gain for me: 0.3 * 0.2 = 0.06). Their messages claim fairness but their bids are essentially all-valueA which is the worst for me. We're very early (t=0.08), so I don't need to concede much. My high-importance issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I'll hold firm on those. I'll continue gradual concessions on low-importance issues (issueA, issueB) while keeping issueD and issueE at valueE. I'll slightly adjust issueC to show movement.

The opponent is stuck at all-valueA bids (utility 0.2 for me) with tiny concessions - they moved issueE from valueA to valueB once, and now moved issueD to valueB. Their messaging claims cooperation but the bids are extremely lopsided in their favor. We're only at t=0.10, so there's no rush. My highest-weight issues are issueD and issueE (0.3 each), so I should hold firm on those. I'll keep issueD and issueE at valueE, and make modest concessions on the lower-weight issues. My last bid was issueA:A, issueB:A, issueC:C, issueD:E, issueE:E (utility = 0.1*0.2 + 0.1*0.2 + 0.2*0.6 + 0.3*1.0 + 0.3*1.0 = 0.02+0.02+0.12+0.3+0.3 = 0.76). I'll keep this general shape but slightly adjust to avoid exact repetition - keep issueD and issueE at E, drop issueC slightly to C or keep, vary issueA/B.

The opponent is essentially stuck near all-valueA bids, making minimal concessions (only issueE moved to valueC in their latest offer, and issueD briefly to valueB). Their utility to me from their latest offer is very low: issueA=0.2*0.1 + issueB=0.2*0.1 + issueC=0.2*0.2 + issueD=0.2*0.3 + issueC=0.6*0.3 = 0.02+0.02+0.04+0.06+0.18 = 0.32. We're only at t=0.12, so there's plenty of time. I'll slightly adjust to vary my bid while maintaining a strong position - I'll keep my most important issues (D and E) high but perhaps nudge issueC slightly, and keep A and B at low values since those are low importance to me.
